ops.title: codex skill reply files in /tmp ($$-suffixed) can collide across concurrent sessions/accounts via PID reuse — verify reply content matches the question before trusting it
ops.body:
During a parallel codex review run (2026-07-12), a codex-wrapper agent read `/tmp/codex-r-11398.txt` and got a different repo's review — a concurrent codex run in another session/account had produced a file under a reused PID. The agent caught it only because the content didn't match its question.

**Why:** the codex skill names reply files with a `$$` (PID) suffix in shared `/tmp`. Same-session parallel calls get unique PIDs, but separate agent processes across sessions/accounts share `/tmp`, and low PIDs get reused — so a stale or foreign reply file can sit at the expected path.

**How to apply:** when running the codex skill or [[codex-skill-stub-from-schema-wrappers]]-style wrappers, always sanity-check that the reply file's content answers the question that was asked (topic match), prefer the session scratchpad dir over `/tmp` for question/reply files when driving `codex exec` manually, and treat a mismatched reply as a collision — re-run rather than salvage.
